models.code:
class Customer(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'customer'
    
    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True, autoincrement=True)
    name = Column(String)
    balance = Column(Decimal)
    credit_limit = Column(Decimal)
models.description: Represents customers in the system with a balance and credit limit.
models.name: Customer

models.code:
class Order(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'order'
    
    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True, autoincrement=True)
    customer_id = Column(ForeignKey('customer.id'))
    date_shipped = Column(DateTime)
    notes = Column(String)
    amount_total = Column(Decimal)
models.description: Represents customer orders including a total amount.
models.name: Order

models.code:
class Item(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'item'
    
    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True, autoincrement=True)
    order_id = Column(ForeignKey('order.id'))
    product_id = Column(ForeignKey('product.id'))
    quantity = Column(Integer)
    unit_price = Column(Decimal)
    amount = Column(Decimal)
models.description: Represents items in each order with calculated amount.
models.name: Item

models.code:
class Product(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'product'
    
    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True, autoincrement=True)
    name = Column(String)
    unit_price = Column(Decimal)
models.name: Product

rules.name: Customer Balance Constraint
rules.description: Ensures a customer's balance does not exceed their credit limit.
rules.code:
Rule.constraint(validate=Customer,
                as_condition=lambda row: row.balance <= row.credit_limit,
                error_msg="Customer balance ({row.balance}) exceeds credit limit ({row.credit_limit})")

rules.name: Order Total Calculation
rules.description: Calculates order total as the sum of its item amounts.

rules.name: Item Amount Calculation
rules.description: Calculates item amount as quantity times unit price.

rules.name: Customer Balance Calculation
rules.description: Derives customer balance as sum of unpaid orders.
rules.code:
Rule.sum(derive=Customer.balance,
             as_sum_of=Order.amount_total,
             where=lambda row: row.date_shipped is None)

rules.name: Item Unit Price Copy
rules.description: Copies product unit price into item unit price.

rules.name: Order Kafka Integration
rules.description: Sends order details to Kafka if the order is shipped.
rules.code:
Rule.after_flush_row_event(on_class=Order, calling=kafka_producer.send_row_to_kafka,
                           if_condition=lambda row: row.date_shipped is not None,
                           with_args={"topic": "order_shipping"})

test_data:
from datetime import date

# Customers
customer1 = Customer(id=1, name="John Doe", balance=Decimal('100.00'), credit_limit=Decimal('500.00'))
customer2 = Customer(id=2, name="Jane Smith", balance=Decimal('200.00'), credit_limit=Decimal('800.00'))
customer3 = Customer(id=3, name="Alice Johnson", balance=Decimal('150.00'), credit_limit=Decimal('750.00'))
customer4 = Customer(id=4, name="Bob Brown", balance=Decimal('120.00'), credit_limit=Decimal('600.00'))

# Products
product1 = Product(id=1, name="Laptop", unit_price=Decimal('1000.00'))
product2 = Product(id=2, name="Mouse", unit_price=Decimal('25.00'))
product3 = Product(id=3, name="Keyboard", unit_price=Decimal('45.00'))
product4 = Product(id=4, name="Monitor", unit_price=Decimal('150.00'))

# Orders
order1 = Order(id=1, customer_id=1, date_shipped=None, notes="Urgent order", amount_total=Decimal('0.00'))
order2 = Order(id=2, customer_id=2, date_shipped=date(2023, 10, 15), notes="Gift order", amount_total=Decimal('0.00'))
order3 = Order(id=3, customer_id=3, date_shipped=None, notes="Bulk purchase", amount_total=Decimal('0.00'))
order4 = Order(id=4, customer_id=4, date_shipped=date(2023, 9, 5), notes="Regular order", amount_total=Decimal('0.00'))

# Items
item1 = Item(id=1, order_id=1, product_id=1, quantity=1, unit_price=Decimal('1000.00'), amount=Decimal('1000.00'))
item2 = Item(id=2, order_id=2, product_id=2, quantity=2, unit_price=Decimal('25.00'), amount=Decimal('50.00'))
item3 = Item(id=3, order_id=3, product_id=3, quantity=3, unit_price=Decimal('45.00'), amount=Decimal('135.00'))
item4 = Item(id=4, order_id=4, product_id=4, quantity=1, unit_price=Decimal('150.00'), amount=Decimal('150.00'))
